revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a game-development pixel-art terrain tile asset sheet.

Canvas and layout:
- Exact image size: 64x64 px.
- 4 rows x 4 columns grid, 16 total frames.
- Each frame is exactly 16x16 px.
- No visible grid lines, no borders, no labels, no tile IDs, no text, no UI.
- Every frame stays fully inside its own 16x16 cell and keeps the same scale.
- Fill all 16 cells edge-to-edge with tile art. If any empty space exists, it must be solid #FF00FF magenta, but terrain should generally fill each cell.

Style:
- Crisp low-resolution SNES / 16-bit pixel art, no antialiasing blur.
- Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, warm golden-hour sunlight, cheerful hand-painted storybook feeling.
- Top-left light source, consistent palette, readable pixel clusters, one-pixel dark outline where useful.
- Top-down world-map terrain view, not isometric.
- Think broad adventure geography, not close-up realistic scale.

Asset concept:
- A winding mountain dirt path autotile set using the curved-edge logic of a coastline autotile preset.
- Material: warm compacted moist earth, slightly lighter worn center line, scattered flat pale stepping stones, small embedded pebbles, delicate grass tufts from micro-cracks.
- Grass-to-path boundary must never be a straight hard line. It always curves, frays, breaks up, or has individual grass-blade intrusions.
- Avoid making every cell a repeated square stamp; vary texture, edge breakup, stone placement, pebble clusters, earth saturation, and lighting while keeping the whole set cohesive.

Height micro-berm rule for edge tiles:
- On each directional edge frame, the named side shows a three-part height detail: bright upper grass lip at the path outer edge, short compacted-earth vertical micro-face about 4-6 pixels thick with embedded pebbles and root wisps, and a soft cast shadow on the lower grass beyond it.
- The opposite side remains pure path interior.
- Boundary must be gently curved and organic, not a straight horizontal or vertical split.

Strict 4x4 layout, row by row:
Row 1, cells 1-4: CENTER walkable path interior variations. Fully walkable warm earth interiors, seamless blend of earth, flat pale stones, faint shoe-tread impressions, pebble clusters. Each cell differs in stone arrangement and earth tone to prevent visible repetition.
Row 2, cells 5-8: DIRECTIONAL EDGES in this exact order: N edge, S edge, W edge, E edge. The named side has the organic grass-invasion boundary plus the bright grass lip, short earth face, and lower grass shadow. The opposite side is path interior.
Row 3, cells 9-12: OUTER CORNERS in this exact order: NW, NE, SW, SE. Each is an organically rounded outer corner where the path turns. The earth boundary forms a gentle quarter-arc with moss and protruding grass blades softening the elbow. Absolutely no 90-degree square corner shapes.
Row 4, cells 13-16: ACCENT / INNER variants. Cell 13: T-junction path-crossing accent. Cell 14: mossy embedded flat stone cluster. Cell 15: wet mud patch with a small rain-pool shine. Cell 16: narrow grass-blade strip cutting diagonally across the path as a grass intrusion accent.

Seam and tile quality:
- Tiles should be usable as an autotile set and read naturally when repeated.
- Curved dirt/grass transitions should blend naturally, not form hard rectangular bands.
- Keep texture low-frequency enough that the final game view does not read as a checkerboard.

Exclusions:
- No copyrighted game characters, logos, recognizable franchise likenesses, letter emblems, watermarks, modern objects, weapons, armor costumes, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, animals, companion creatures, humans, characters, or UI elements.

Output should be a single crisp high-resolution rendering of the exact 64x64 pixel-art sheet, preserving hard pixel edges and the exact 4x4 16px-cell structure without drawing the grid.
